# Repl save and exit integration (2026-03-13 06:23Z)

_**User**_

Implement Task 17 from docs/plans/2026-03-13-dev-shell-impl-plan.md.

**Task 17: save / exit 与 REPL 集成**

1. In src/command.rs add two builtins if not present:
   - "save" [path]: call serialization::save_to_file(vfs, path). Path default e.g. ".dev_shell.bin" or take from argv[1]. Return error if path missing and no default, or save fails.
   - "exit" and "quit": do not run a normal command; return a special result (e.g. enum RunResult { Continue, Exit }) so the REPL can break the loop.

2. Create src/repl.rs. In src/lib.rs add: pub mod repl;
   - repl::run(vfs: &mut Vfs, stdin: impl Read, stdout: impl Write, stderr: impl Write) -> Result<(), ()> or similar.
   - Loop: read a line from stdin (e.g. BufRead::read_line), trim. If empty or only whitespace, continue. Parse with parser::parse_line; if parse error, write to stderr and continue.
   - If the (first) command is "exit" or "quit" (argv[0]), set a flag and break out of the loop (don't call execute_pipeline for that line).
   - Otherwise build ExecContext with vfs and stdin/stdout/stderr, call command::execute_pipeline(ctx, &pipeline). On error write to stderr and continue.
   - Optional: on exit, if unsaved prompt (design said optional), print a message; for now just exit.

3. Run cargo test. Commit: git add src/repl.rs src/command.rs src/lib.rs && git commit -m "feat(repl): loop with parse and execute, save/exit"

Work directory: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/dev_shell
Report: how exit/save are handled, REPL loop, test result, commit.
